August 2I,2006
John R. Harper, City Attomey
City of Murrieta
Harper & Bums LLP
453 S. Glassell Street
Orange, California 92866
RE: Your Request for Informal Assistance
Our FiIe No. W-06-161
Dear Mr. Harper:
This letter is in response to your request for informal assistance, on behalf of the
City of Murrieta Councilmember Warnie Enochs, regarding the conflict-of-interest
provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").1 The Act requires the Commission to
provide written advice, including informal assistance, to any person whose duties under
the Act are in question or to that person's authorized representative. (Section 8311a@):
regulation 18329(c)(1).) However, we may decline to provide informal assistance if it
would be inappropriate or otherwise not in the public interest to do so. (Regulation
1832e(c)($.)
Based on facts provided in a telephone conversation on August 17,2006, we
decline to provide informal assistance to you at this time. Your current question involves
an analysis of a series of interrelated decisions, some of which have already beeri made.
Issuing informal assistance at this time would relate to past conduct and, therefore, would
not further the impartial, effective administration of the Act so mandated by section
831 I 1.
Therefore, your request is withdrawn. I enclose a copy of regulation 18329 for
your review.
I Government Code sections 8 1000 - 91014. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections
18109-18997, of the California Code ofRegulations.

If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.
Sincerely,
John Wallace
Acting General Counsel
By: Brian G. Lau
Counsel, Legal Division

sections.conclusion: Based on facts provided in a telephone conversation on August 17, 2006, the FPPC declines to provide informal assistance. The request is withdrawn because issuing informal assistance would relate to past conduct and would not further the impartial, effective administration of the Act as mandated by section 83111.
sections.question_synthetic: Whether Councilmember Warnie Enochs of the City of Murrieta has a conflict of interest under the Political Reform Act regarding a series of interrelated decisions, some of which have already been made.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC declines to provide informal assistance because the question involves analysis of past decisions already made, which would not further the effective administration of the Political Reform Act.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Declination/withdrawal letter with clear conclusion section. No formal QUESTION section header present but question can be synthesized from the RE line and body text. Document explicitly states 'your request is withdrawn.'

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether Councilmember Warnie Enochs of the City of Murrieta has a conflict of interest under the Political Reform Act regarding a series of interrelated decisions, some of which have already been made.

CONCLUSION: Based on facts provided in a telephone conversation on August 17, 2006, the FPPC declines to provide informal assistance. The request is withdrawn because issuing informal assistance would relate to past conduct and would not further the impartial, effective administration of the Act as mandated by section 83111.

embedding.summary: FPPC declines to provide informal assistance to the City Attorney regarding a conflict-of-interest question for Councilmember Warnie Enochs, finding that the analysis of already-completed interrelated decisions would not serve the Act's purposes.
